net_exit_meaning: 0 only when EVERY case below classifies as expected; any misclassification -> 1
verdict_line_expected: === app_adapter_fixture_suite: PASS (green + I5A positive + 19 verify negatives (N/I/M) + 4 checker tamper negatives all classify) ===
label_discrepancy_finding: The verdict string says '19 verify negatives' but the suite actually enumerates 24 verify-fail negatives (12 N + 5 I + 7 M). '19' is the stale pre-A-2 count (the closeout report, V2.6_CLOSEOUT_REPORT.md sec.6 item 5, records the suite as 19/19 at the Phase-A hold; N13 + I1-I5 + M1-M7 were added in A-2 and the verdict label was never updated). COSMETIC ONLY: exit code is 0 and all 24 negatives + 4 tamper + 3 green + 1 positive classify correctly (confirmed in run_logs_session2_green/net_app_adapter_fixture_suite.txt). Carried as an informational finding for the 3.0 port to correct the label; NOT a baseline defect.
fixture_determinism_note: Fixtures are generated per-run by New-FixtureTree/Write-TsDict. Write-TsDict groups nested keys via an UNORDERED hashtable ($groups=@{}), and command_evidence/APP_END_EVIDENCE timestamps are injected from Get-Date. Therefore fixture BYTES are NOT run-stable and MUST NOT be the replay comparison target. The frozen comparison target is the (case-id -> expected exit + expected check-ID) tuple set below, which IS deterministic. See NORMALIZED_REPLAY_DESIGN.md.
